predictedBugs: detectSoloDeveloper uses execSync (sync) - if git binary is missing or permission denied, catch falls back to not-solo (safe default).

When user accepts solo-hardened switch, reviewMode is updated in-memory but solo-hardened section header is skipped via soloHeaderShown flag. Inline duplicate code must stay in sync with the main block.
Multiple email aliases for same developer could falsely show 2+ committers, triggering team enforcement when actually solo. Git shortlog groups by email identity.
edgeCases: Empty git (no commits in 30 days): shortlog returns empty, committerCount=0, isSolo=true -> solo prompt shown. Correct for new solo project.
Git not available / not a git repo: error caught, isSolo=false (safe default), falls through to normal C12 block. Correct denial.
Non-interactive mode (CI, no TTY): confirmOrExit returns false without --yes flag, so solo switch is declined. --yes flag overrides this.
--approve not yet passed when solo prompt triggers: the flow continues through rest of gatekeep. If --approve not given, the No decision flag check still applies.
